pages.full_text:
[Ticket 1 — printed] CONWY VALLEY RAILWAY MUSEUM / BETWS-Y-COED / VISITOR'S TICKET / CHILD / Valid only for day of issue / [serial no.] 7643
[Ticket 2 — printed] CONWY VALLEY RAILWAY MUSEUM / BETWS-Y-COED / MINIATURE RAILWAY / Valid only for one journey / Issued subject to the Company's regs. / [serial no.] 8187
[Postcard caption — printed] THE CONWY VALLEY   RAILWAY, BETWS-Y-COED
pages.notes: Page consists entirely of two glued-in railway tickets (printed) and a glued-in postcard of the Conwy Valley Railway at Betws-y-Coed. No handwritten page number visible. The right gutter shows partial handwritten text but this belongs to the right page.

pages.full_text:
Stone farmhouse bottom of valley.   R. Alwen  s.e of Betws-y-Coed.
Brick porch with wooden 'railway front.'   Dolgungles

   We left Peter and Clare at the riding school. We drove back through __Betws-y-Coed__. Touristy shop + a lovely miniature railway which William, John + Charles went on. Very friendly staff. We looked around the museum, it even had the old smell of a ticket office. We saw the old church - lit only by candles, ceiling falling down. (B Old font, [illegible] with 2 carveson each face.
Charles 6.day cake.
pages.notes: Page includes a hand-drawn pen sketch of a stone farmhouse with trees, captioned 'Stone farmhouse bottom of valley. R. Alwen s.e of Betws-y-Coed. Brick porch with wooden railway front. Dolgungles'. 'Dolgungles' may be a mis-spelling or phonetic rendering of a Welsh place name near the R. Alwen; could read 'Dolgynlas' or similar — transcribed as written. Bottom lines are partially cut off/cramped; '(B Old font, [illegible] with 2 carveson each face' — the bracketed opening and word after 'font' are unclear; 'carveson' may be 'carves on'. 'Charles 6.day cake' is a note at the bottom left, possibly 'Charles 6 day cake' (a birthday note). The page number '38' is visible in the top-right corner.
